violations.text: No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. PIC license recently expired. Per PIC, new class will be taken. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

Raw animal foods (shaved steak) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (lettuce) in the reach-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Guidance document provided and must be placed on fridge to allow staff to view the proper storage. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. Per PIC, new menus will be printed and it will be added. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026
No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

Soap was not available at both hand-washing sinks in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Soap was placed at hand sinks. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026
One of the hand-washing sinks in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

Raw animal foods (shaved steak) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (lettuce) in the refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Proper storage guidance document provided. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Can opener taken to 3-bay. Slicer taken out of service. Proper sanitizing reviewed with PIC. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

Various foods were observed held above 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Lasagna was observed above 45 degrees and was stored in unit since yesterday, product was disposed of. All other product below 45 was relocated to other refrigeration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

(Chemical) was stored with food, equipment, utensils, or single service items. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. Product relocated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

Utensils are displayed with the lip surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees and consumers if consumer self-service is provided. Employee fixed utensils. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

Both Pepsi coolers were observed unable to maintain a temperature of 41 or below to keep food at an adequate temperature. Units must be used for non TCS foods only until adjusted, serviced, or repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the shelving of refrigeration equipment have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. All units must be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. Guidance document provided. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026
The door to the toilet room is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

The pre-flush spray hose at the 3-bay is hanging below the flood rim. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/19/2024

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/19/2024

Raw animal foods were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods in the reach-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Foods were relocated. PIC to instruct all employees of proper food storage. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2024

Food contact surfaces of the slicer were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Proper sanitizing procedure reviewed with PIC. PIC to instruct all staff of proper sanitizing. Slicer to be broken down and sanitized before next use. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2024

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen/prep area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2024

The pre-flush spray hose at the 3-bay sink is hanging below the flood rim. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2024

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2024

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/12/2024

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arrranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/12/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer to be broken down and sanitized prior to use. New Violation. Correct By: 11/12/2024

Thermometers are lacking in the refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 11/12/2024

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 11/12/2024

The presence of flying insects is not controlled as evidenced by accumulation of flying insects. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 11/12/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the equipment have an accumulation of dust, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/12/2024

The pre-flush spray hose at the 3-bay sink is hanging below the flood rim. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/12/2024

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/12/2024
The door to the toilet room that opens up to food storage is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 11/12/2024

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Soap was supplied at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/05/2023

At the time of inspection, raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination in the 2 door reach-in cooler in the cookline area... - Packages of beef were observed stored next to and below raw chicken and eggs. - Raw fish was observed stored above previously shredded cheese Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. PIC began moving food items at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/05/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. At the time of inspection, previously cooked pasta was placed hot in a densely packed refrigerator on a pan wrapped covered tightly with plastic wrap. Foods set for cooling are to be arranged in the EQUIPMENT to provide maximum heat transfer through the container walls and loosely covered, or uncovered if protected from overhead contamination. Pasta was moved to the top area of the cooler unit and uncovered at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/05/2023
The faucet to the mope sink was observed to be broken/in poor repair. Facility equipment is to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/19/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the following were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris... - Refrigeration surfaces internal/external - Prep table shelving and legs - cookline equipment surfaces between units Non-FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to preclude accumulation of soil residues. New Violation. Correct By: 10/09/2023

The pre-flush spray at the 3 bay sink is hanging below the flood rim. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. New Violation. Correct By: 10/19/2023

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. Air gap is to be installed. New Violation. Correct By: 10/19/2023

The following areas of the "Physical Facilities" were observed to be in poor repair... - Several floor tiles in the kitchen area were observed to be cracked/chipped/missing - Joints of the plastic board wall covering were observed to be loosely applied/separating The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2023

The floor and walls in the kitchen and prep areas were observed to have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/19/2023
